records.ety: [Hind. jangal desert, forest, jungle; Skr. jagala desert.]

defs: A dense growth of brushwood, grasses, reeds, vines, etc.; an almost impenetrable thicket of trees, canes, and reedy vegetation, as in India, Africa, Australia, and Brazil.
quotes: The jungles of India are of bamboos, canes, and other palms, very difficult to penetrate.

defs: A place of danger or ruthless competition for survival.
Anything which causes confusion or difficulty due to intricacy; as, a jungle of environmental regulations.
notes: The latter, which resembles the domestic gamecock, is supposed to be one of the original species from which the domestic fowl was derived.
